calendar.matters:
Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-03-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lon Lazar for Mary Narron.

Mr. Lazar stated that he filed a brief on 4/8/19 in support of the petition and presented brief argument. Discussions follow. Mr. Lazar then stated that the Court has the power to move venue, that the only issue would be with regard to the Trustee, that the Trustee has abandoned her position, that she has cut off communication with the sole beneficiary (his client), that she won't respond to him, and that she resides in Huntington Beach. The Court inquired if the Trust was being administered in Huntington Beach. Mr. Lazar stated that it was not being administered in Huntington Beach, that it was originally administered out-of-state, that once the interest in the Shasta property was distributed the Trustee left, that the petitioner is the sole beneficiary at this point, that she does business here in San Francisco, that the property has a buyer & can be sold, and that his client just needs to be appointed. Mr. Lazar also stated that his client requests that the Court appoint her so she can take care of the business, that she doesn't know any attorneys in Shasta County, that she lives between here and San Francisco, and that she does business in San Francisco. The Court stated that it doesn't like to take things just for the convenience of someone, that it puts more on this court's Probate Examiners, that there could be issues that may arise down the road re: witnesses (etc.), and that the code is clear. Mr. Lazar concluded by stating that his client does business here, that he is her attorney, and that the Trust would be administered through the San Francisco business office. At the conclusion of counsel's argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Chan. Matter not reported.

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-18-2019 Continued To Apr-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
